items.headline: Wyatt Johnston thriving on power play for Dallas in postseason
items.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Wyatt Johnston quietly stationed himself below the left face-off circle with the Dallas Stars on the power play in double overtime Wednesday, his eyes fixed on the puck as it went from Miro Heiskanen at the point to Jason Robertson on the near half-wall and back

items.headline: Ottawa Senators Jake Sanderson leaves Game 3 with injury
items.description:
OTTAWA -- Jake Sanderson left in the second period for the Ottawa Senators in a 2-1 loss to the Carolina Hurricanes in Game 3 of the Eastern Conference First Round at Canadian Tire Centre on Thursday.
Sanderson, Ottawa's top defenseman, was hit in the head by Hurricanes forward Taylor Hall

items.description:

items.headline: Carolina Hurricanes Ottawa Senators Game 3 recap April 23 2026
items.description: OTTAWA -- Logan Stankoven opened the scoring for the third straight game for the Carolina Hurricanes, who took a 3-0 series lead against the Ottawa Senators with a 2-1 win in Game 3 of the Eastern Conference First Round at Canadian Tire Centre on Thursday.​

items.headline: 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs first round schedule television results
items.description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs are underway. Here is the complete schedule for the first round of the playoffs, including TV information and results:

items.headline: Buffalo Sabres Boston Bruins Game 3 recap April 23 2026
items.description: BOSTON -- Alex Tuch scored the go-ahead goal early in the third period for the Buffalo Sabres, who defeated the Boston Bruins 3-1 in Game 3 of the Eastern Conference First Round at TD Garden on Thursday.

items.headline: Alex Ovechkin Capitals Hershey Bears playoffs
items.description:
Talk about a Great fan.
Washington Capitals captain Alex Ovechkin, aka The Great Eight, was in attendance for the Hershey Bears Calder Cup Playoff game on Thursday.
Ovechkin, the NHL's career goals leader, took in Game 2 of the Best of 3 Atlantic Division First Round series between the Bears

items.headline: Corey Perry ready to play villain for Lightning in Game 3
items.description:
MONTREAL -- Want to make Corey Perry smile?
Boo him.
Loudly. Lustily. With passion. Like you mean it.
Just like the capacity crowd of 21,105 at raucous Bell Centre will be doing, in full collective throats, for Game 3 of the Eastern Conference First Round between Perry’s Tampa Bay Lightning

items.headline: St Louis Blues Jordan Kyrou injury status April 23 2026
items.description: Jordan Kyrou had a minor knee procedure this week but is expected to be ready for training camp with the St. Louis Blues.

items.headline: Travis Sanheim 'workhorse' with Philadelphia on cusp of 2nd round
items.description:
VOORHEES, N.J. -- Travis Sanheim skated onto the ice at Flyers Training Center on Thursday with the rest of his Philadelphia Flyers teammates, went through all the drills and never looked tired or taxed.

items.headline: Dallas Stars Minnesota Wild thankful for extra day rest before Game 4
items.description:
MINNEAPOLIS -- There was a group of players on the floor stretching, others finishing up a late breakfast, and some walking around the hotel lobby on their phones, heading outside for a light walk on a cloudy, misty Minneapolis day.

items.headline: Flyers Martone Zegras have top-selling jerseys of NHL playoffs
items.description:
Martone Mania has hit Philadelphia and the NHL.

items.headline: Montreal rallying around Kirby Dach heading into Game 3 vs Tampa Bay
items.description:
BROSSARD, Quebec -- The Montreal Canadiens demonstrated their pack mentality by standing together to defend Kirby Dach in the blame game.
The Canadiens center iced the puck in overtime and then lost coverage on J.J. Moser off the ensuing face-off before the Tampa Bay Lightning defenseman scored to give his

items.headline: Dan Muse Muse leans on experience to get Pittsburgh out of hole against Philadelphia
items.description:
PHILADELPHIA -- Dan Muse already performed one impressive feat getting the Pittsburgh Penguins to the Stanley Cup Playoffs in what many believed would be a rebuilding season.
For his next trick, though, the Penguins coach will need to do something historic.
